overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a focused and professional social media presence across Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, primarily sharing academic research, project updates, and career opportunities in computational social science and disinformation studies. With a combined total of 3,580 followers, the engagement rate averages around 5.32% per post. LinkedIn contributes the largest audience, while Bluesky demonstrates the highest average engagement per post, indicating a highly active audience for research-related content.

contentThemes.description: Announcements of new papers, research project releases (e.g., CooRnet v2.0), and successful grant applications, often focused on disinformation and coordinated behavior.
Posts related to hiring postdoctoral researchers, PhD student opportunities, and general calls for interested computational social scientists.
Sharing upcoming presentations at academic conferences, highlighting specific computational methods or research areas.
Discussions on methodological approaches to studying complex social phenomena like disinformation across multiple platforms.

recommendations: Cross-promote major announcements (e.g., new papers, grant awards, hiring) across all platforms, tailoring the message to each platform's audience and format. This can amplify reach and engagement.

Utilize LinkedIn's strong engagement for professional announcements like grants and hiring, as this content performs very well there. Consider adding rich media (images, short videos) to further boost visibility.

On Bluesky and Mastodon, continue sharing granular research updates, methodological discussions, and calls for academic collaboration. Experiment with asking open-ended questions to foster more discussion in comments/replies.
Regularly include clear calls-to-action in posts (e.g., 'Read the paper here', 'Apply now', 'Join the discussion') to guide followers to desired outcomes and track specific engagement metrics.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky shows the highest average engagement rate (6.14%) per post, particularly for announcements of new publications and research opportunities. Its audience seems highly receptive to direct academic updates and calls for collaboration, making it a valuable platform for community building and talent attraction.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon serves as a good platform for specific research tool updates (like CooRnet) and academic discussions, with a moderate engagement rate (4.55%). Its audience appears to be concentrated on in-depth methodological content. Consistency in sharing niche academic insights could further cultivate this dedicated following.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn boasts the largest follower count (1850) and strong engagement for professional news, such as grant awards and hiring calls (average 4.87%). This platform is excellent for broader professional networking, attracting talent, and showcasing significant research milestones to a wider professional and academic audience.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
Our team has just released v2.0 of CooRnet, with improved algorithms for detecting coordinated link sharing behavior.
Looking forward to presenting at #DisinfoConf2025 next month on computational methods for detecting coordinated campaigns
